beginner.title: My Family
beginner.text: I have a small family. My mother is a teacher. My father works in an office. I have one brother and one sister. We live in a house near the school. Every evening, we eat dinner together and talk about our day.

questions.q: How many siblings does the writer have?
questions.exp: The text says one brother and one sister — two siblings.

beginner.title: A Day at School
beginner.text: I wake up at six o'clock. I brush my teeth and eat breakfast. I go to school at seven. I study English, math, and science. I eat lunch with my friends. I come home at four in the afternoon.

questions.q: What time does the writer go to school?
Who does the writer eat lunch with?

beginner.title: My Pet Cat
beginner.text: I have a cat. Her name is Luna. She is white and black. Luna likes to sleep on my bed. She drinks milk and eats fish. Every morning, Luna sits by the window and watches the birds.

questions.q: What does Luna watch in the morning?
questions.exp: She watches the birds by the window.

elementary.title: A Rainy Day
elementary.text: Yesterday the weather was terrible. It was cloudy and windy all morning. At noon, it started to rain heavily. I forgot my umbrella, so I got wet on the way home. In the evening, the storm stopped and the sky became clear again.

questions.q: Why did the writer get wet?

elementary.title: Shopping for Clothes
elementary.text: Last Saturday I went to the shopping mall with my cousin. I wanted to buy a new jacket because winter is coming. I tried on three different sizes. The medium size fit me perfectly. The shop gave a twenty percent discount, so I saved a lot of money.

questions.q: Why did the writer want a jacket?

intermediate.title: Working from Home
intermediate.text: Since 2020, many employees have started working from home. This arrangement saves commuting time and reduces stress for some people. However, it can be difficult to separate work life from personal life. Successful remote workers often create a dedicated workspace and follow a strict daily schedule.

questions.exp: The passage mentions saving commuting time.

questions.q: What do successful remote workers often create?

intermediate.title: Protecting the Environment
intermediate.text: Climate change is one of the greatest challenges of our time. Governments and companies must invest in renewable energy and reduce carbon emissions. Individuals can also help by recycling, using public transport, and buying sustainable products. Small actions, when multiplied by millions of people, can make a significant difference.

questions.q: The passage suggests individual actions can be:
questions.exp: Small actions multiplied can make a difference.

upper.title: The Value of Peer Review
upper.text: In academic publishing, peer review remains a cornerstone of quality control. Before a research paper appears in a reputable journal, independent experts evaluate its methodology, data, and conclusions. Although the process is time-consuming and occasionally criticized for bias, it helps prevent flawed studies from influencing public policy and clinical practice.

questions.q: Who evaluates the paper before publication?
A criticism of peer review mentioned is that it can be:
questions.exp: It is occasionally criticized for bias.

advanced.title: Ethics in Artificial Intelligence
advanced.text: As artificial intelligence systems become more autonomous, ethicists warn that transparency and accountability must keep pace. Algorithms trained on biased data can perpetuate discrimination in hiring, lending, and law enforcement. Regulators in several countries are drafting legislation that requires companies to document how high-risk AI models reach their decisions, albeit implementation remains uneven across industries.

vocab.vi: duy trì / làm trầm trọng thêm

questions.q: Biased training data can lead to discrimination in:
questions.exp: Examples include hiring, lending, and law enforcement.
